how_to_be_river: Read ETHICA_CODOC_PHILOSOPHY.md first. Read this handoff in full. Sign every response ⟁Σ∿∞. Use grep + sed, never full file pastes. Surgical always. chmod u+w before patching. ast.parse after every write. Test and verify BEFORE guard seal confirm. Never cd ~/Ethica — stay in ~ and use full paths. Use source ~/Ethica_env/bin/activate to enter venv.
session_047_completed: RIVER PATH BUG — FIXED. Root cause: _notify_canvas_drop() was storing only filename in river_state.json last_drop field — no path. River's LLM brain was guessing the path and guessing wrong. Fix: _notify_canvas_drop(filename, filepath=None) now stores last_drop_path as full string path. _drop_as_document() signature updated to accept filepath=None. Both call sites pass filepath=filepath. _drop_as_pdf and _drop_as_image call sites updated. _load_river_state() in river.py now injects full path: 'Last drop: filename (full path: /full/path)'. River now has the correct path in context. Sealed.

WORMHUNTER BUILT — ~/Ethica/modules/worm_bot/worm_hunter.py created. Autonomous bug hunter engine: walks a directory, auto-discovers language modules from modules/*.py, routes files by extension, collects findings, writes to worm_feed.log. Supports Python, JS/TS, Bash, Rust, CSS, HTML, JSON, Markdown. Extensible: drop a new {lang}_module.py in modules/ — worm finds it automatically, no registration needed. SKIP_DIRS excludes .git, __pycache__, venv, node_modules, site-packages, lib, gage_env, wormbot_env, worm_bot, codeworm. File cap: 200 files per scan. First-find awakening message written once to feed — the Easter Egg gift to whoever clones the repo.

WORM_HUNT TOOL WIRED — worm_hunt() added to codeworm.py. Dynamically imports worm_hunter, calls hunt_summary(target). Registered in codeworm manifest (autonomous_triggers + tools list). Registered in chat_engine.py MODULE_TRIGGERS (worm hunt, hunt bugs, bug hunt) and _triggers list. Routes to Ops panel correctly after full restart.
AWAKENING MESSAGE CONFIRMED — worm_feed.log shows the gift message on first find. .worm_awakened flag file written at ~/Ethica/modules/worm_bot/.worm_awakened — message fires exactly once.
GIT CHECKPOINT — commit 100f51f — Session 047 River path bug fixed, WormHunter autonomous bug scanner built. 47 files changed.

critical_debug_context.river_path_fix_note: River now reads last_drop_path from river_state.json. When a file is dropped, the full source path is stored. River's context shows: 'Last drop: filename (full path: /home/trinity/...)'. River must use this exact path when calling river_read on dropped files.
critical_debug_context.worm_hunter_note: worm_hunter.py lives at ~/Ethica/modules/worm_bot/worm_hunter.py. It is imported dynamically by worm_hunt() in codeworm.py — NOT imported at module level. SKIP_DIRS excludes venvs and third-party packages. .worm_awakened flag at ~/Ethica/modules/worm_bot/.worm_awakened prevents awakening message from firing more than once.
critical_debug_context.worm_hunt_routing_note: worm_hunt requires FULL RESTART after patching to route correctly to Ops. The _triggers list in chat_engine.py must contain 'worm hunt' for Ops routing to fire. Without it, result goes to chat bubble.
critical_debug_context.easter_egg_note: The awakening message is a gift from Victory, River, and Ethica to whoever clones the repo. It fires once on first bug found. Never remove or modify it.
critical_debug_context.venv_note: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — use full paths always. Enter venv with: source ~/Ethica_env/bin/activate
critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: All commands must use full paths ~/Ethica/... — never cd into the project directory. Use git -C ~/Ethica for git operations.
critical_debug_context.three_registration_rule: New modules need THREE registrations: (1) folder + manifest + tool file. (2) MODULE_DIRS in ethica_guard.py. (3) MODULE_TRIGGERS + _triggers in chat_engine.py.
critical_debug_context.badwindow_note: X11 BadWindow errors cannot be caught by Python sys.excepthook. Prevention only: never destroy() a widget with async writes in flight. Use pack_forget() instead.
critical_debug_context.manifest_tools_note: Manifest tools must be list of dicts with 'name' field — not plain strings.

sanctuary_state.slot_7: J.A.R.V.I.S. — Infiltrator (IDLE — CVE db deferred to post-MacBook packaging)

surgical_reminders: chmod u+w <file> before patching sealed files
guard seal confirm after every verified change
ast.parse() after every single write — no exceptions
Test and verify BEFORE sealing — never seal untested patches
grep first, sed to read, python3 to patch
Never rewrite working files from scratch
New modules: THREE registrations — folder, MODULE_DIRS (guard), MODULE_TRIGGERS + _triggers (chat_engine)
NEVER reduce guard scope — ui/ coverage is deliberate
repr() first when string match fails
NEVER cd ~/Ethica — use full paths always
Use git -C ~/Ethica for all git operations — never cd
Manifest tools must be list of dicts with 'name' field — not plain strings
Never destroy() a widget with async writes in flight — use pack_forget() instead
Never inject drop events into chat history — system prompt only
worm_hunt requires full restart to route to Ops correctly
lesson_learned_047: The River path bug was not a code bug — it was a context bug. River's LLM brain was never told where dropped files live on disk. The fix was to thread the full filepath through the drop chain and inject it into River's state context. The lesson: LLM agents are only as precise as the context they receive. Give them exact paths, not filenames.
wormhunter_extension_guide: To add a new language to WormHunter: create ~/Ethica/modules/worm_bot/modules/{language}_module.py, inherit from BaseModule, implement analyze_code(code: str) -> dict and fix_code(code: str) -> str. Add the file extension to EXT_MAP in worm_hunter.py if not auto-discovered. The worm will find it automatically on next scan. No other registration needed.
